title: Print Files

Print Files

Print Files

A print file is created by Uniface using the specified print job model and print mode.

Depending on the settings in the print job model, the file can be temporary—it is removed after being sent to the printer or print management facility—or it is written to disk.

If the Print Queue in the print job model is empty, the print file is written to the current account or directory, or wherever you have assigned such files in the application's assignment file.

The name of the temporary file is the CompoentName.Suffix , where Suffix is an operating system-specific suffix, .pnn or .prt;n , and n is a sequential number.

The first one is called ComponentName.P00, the second ComponentName.P01, and so on, up to ComponentName.P99. The next print file you create will overwrite the print file with the oldest write time.

The file name is returned in $result.

Distinguishing Between the Print Files of Different Clients

For thin clients (such as Uniface Anywhere) that use a common assignment file or a common project directory to locate the print files, the print files may get mixed together with no way of distinguishing between the print files of different clients. To avoid this problem, use the /PID option in the Print Options field of the Define/Edit Print Job Model dialog. Uniface creates a file name for each print file incorporating Uniface's process Id, in the following format (on MS Windows):

Formname ProcessId.Pnn

Note: After Uniface creates a file with this naming format, it strips out the /PID option, so it won't interfere with any parameters you might have specified.

Preventing Files From Being Overwritten

On a FAT file system, print files may be written so quickly (for instance, in a tight loop) that the write time of each file does not change and the same file is repeatedly overwritten. To prevent this, use the assignment setting $PRT_FILE_DELAY to slow the pace of creating print files. For more information, see $PRT_FILE_DELAY.

File Names if Print Mode is Screen

If Print Mode is Screen and you print to a file, the name of the print file created is screen.pnn , where nn is a sequential number starting at 00.

File Names for Printed Fields

When printing the contents of a single field, the name of the print file created is zoom.pnn , where nn is a sequential number.

Related Links
  • $PRT_FILE_DELAY

    Set the delay time for creating print files.

  • Print Job Models

    A print job model is a named collection of printer attributes that determine how and where Uniface forms or reports are printed, including details of the device type, margins, and so on. A print job model must be available before you can print.

  • Print Modes

    Print modes determine how much of a form component and its data is to be printed.

  • Print Queue

    The Print Queue specifies the destination of the printed output.
